title: Locking Behavior With an Entity Service
breadcrumbs: Locking Behavior With an Entity Service
text: Locking Behavior With an Entity Service When a component entity access the database via and entity service, the data is locked by the entity service. The entity service has the same locking behavior as a normal database entity, except when cautious locking is used. In this case the ulock operation in the entity service is activated by the lock statement of a component. For entity services, optimistic locking works in the same way as for other components. Paranoid locking is run using the read/lock command in the entity service. For cautious locking, the ulock operation in the entity service is activated by the lock statement of a component. All fields of the occurrence are locked because the ulock operation usually contains the lock statement. Figure 1. Locking Through an Entity Service When a lock is requested: The lock trigger in the component is activated. The lock trigger contains a lock statement, which activates the entity service's ulock operation. The entity service locks causes the DBMS connector to lock the row in the database. Related Links Optimistic Locking Optimistic locking postpones locking the occurrence until the modified data is actually stored. This causes the lock to be applied at the last possible moment, which can result in considerable performance improvements in multiuser environments, especially for On-line Transaction Processing (OLTP). Cautious Locking Cautious locking causes an occurrence to be locked at the time that a user makes the first modification to a database field in the entity, or when an erase statement is issued in a valid context. (For erase to be valid, the data must come from the database.) Cautious locking is the default Uniface locking method. Paranoid Locking Paranoid locking causes the occurrence to be locked as soon as it is read. This is achieved with the read/lock statement. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
